title: Execute AGV Command To Complete An AGV Task After A Missed RMS Callback
summary: Use the Aveva-exposed Execute AGV command as a recovery mechanism when RMS crashes or fails to send the callback for an AGV task that has already completed physically. The user specifies the full AGV ID so the software can complete the AGV task.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when an AGV has already completed its task physically, but RMS failed to send or process the completion callback, including RMS crash-related recovery described in the source.
not_for: Do not use when the correct full AGV ID is not known.
Do not use to complete a task that has not already completed physically.
Do not treat this runbook as a restart procedure; the source only provides a related safety note that the system should be E-stopped before doing a restart.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the recovery action in the Aveva interface or API area, identify the affected AGV, enter the full AGV ID, and execute the command.

safety_notes: This source describes the action as a recovery mechanism.
Related safety note from the same source context: the system should be E-stopped before doing a restart.
The source does not provide operator guardrails for broader use of this recovery action; keep execution limited to support personnel.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Aveva interface or API page with AGV commands
Knowledge of which AGV task already completed physically

access_required: Access to the Aveva interface or API page with AGV commands
system_preconditions: An AGV task has already completed physically.
RMS failed to send or process the completion callback for that AGV task.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the AGVs Aveva API page and describing exposed AGV command functions and recovery actions, including Execute AGV command.

steps.title: Open the AGVs Aveva API area
steps.purpose: Access the interface area where the AGV recovery command is available.
steps.instruction: Open the Aveva interface or API area where AGV-related commands are available.
steps.expected_result: The AGV-related Aveva API page is open and available for use.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGVs Aveva API page is visible and shows AGV command functions.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV-related Aveva API area cannot be accessed or the expected AGV command page is not available.

actions.label: Open AGVs Aveva API page
actions.exact_action: Open the Aveva interface or API area where AGV-related commands are available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGVs Aveva API page showing exposed AGV command functions and recovery actions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGVs Aveva API page cannot be located or accessed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Selected Geek+ RMS features were incorporated into the Aveva API for ease of use and continuity; the AGVs Aveva API page includes AGV control and recovery functions.

steps.title: Locate Execute AGV command
steps.purpose: Find the specific recovery mechanism used for missed RMS callbacks.
steps.instruction: Locate the recovery mechanism named Execute AGV command.
steps.expected_result: The Execute AGV command input or control is identified on the AGVs Aveva API page.
steps.healthy_condition: The Execute AGV command is visible as an available recovery function.
steps.failure_condition: The Execute AGV command cannot be found on the available AGV command page.

actions.label: Find Execute AGV command
actions.exact_action: Locate the recovery mechanism named Execute AGV command.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Recovery functions on the AGVs Aveva API page, including Execute AGV command.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Execute AGV command is not present where expected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of an RMS crash, Execute AGV command enables users to specify an AGV ID that has completed its task when RMS fails to send a callback.

steps.title: Identify the affected AGV
steps.purpose: Ensure the recovery action is applied to the AGV whose task already completed physically but whose completion callback was missed.
steps.instruction: Identify the AGV that has already completed its task but did not get its completion callback processed by RMS.
steps.expected_result: A single affected AGV is identified for recovery.
steps.healthy_condition: The support user knows which AGV physically completed the task and requires software completion recovery.
steps.failure_condition: The affected AGV cannot be determined or there is uncertainty about whether the task actually completed physically.

actions.label: Confirm affected AGV
actions.exact_action: Identify the AGV that has already completed its task but did not get its completion callback processed by RMS.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct AGV is not known.
It is not confirmed that the AGV task already completed physically.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Execute AGV command is used when RMS fails to send a callback for an AGV ID that has completed its task.

steps.title: Enter the full AGV ID
steps.purpose: Provide the exact AGV identifier required by the recovery command.
steps.instruction: Specify the full AGV ID in the Execute AGV command input.
steps.expected_result: The full AGV ID is entered into the Execute AGV command input.
steps.healthy_condition: The full AGV ID is available and entered exactly as required.
steps.failure_condition: The full AGV ID is unknown, incomplete, or cannot be entered.

actions.label: Input full AGV ID
actions.exact_action: Specify the full AGV ID in the Execute AGV command input.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Execute AGV command area referenced by the training slide; use the input associated with AGV recovery actions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The full AGV ID is not known.
There is uncertainty about whether the entered AGV ID is correct.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The user specifies the full AGV ID in Execute AGV command to complete the task after a missed callback.

steps.title: Execute the recovery command
steps.purpose: Complete the AGV task in software after the missed callback condition.
steps.instruction: Execute the command so the software completes the AGV task.
steps.expected_result: The AGV task is completed in software using the specified full AGV ID.
steps.healthy_condition: The software completes the AGV task for the specified AGV ID after the missed callback condition.
steps.failure_condition: The command does not complete the AGV task as expected.

actions.label: Run Execute AGV command
actions.exact_action: Execute the command so the software completes the AGV task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV recovery command area associated with Execute AGV command on the AGVs Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: The command does not complete the task as expected.
The resulting software state does not reflect task completion for the specified AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Execute AGV command enables users to specify an AGV ID that has completed its task when RMS fails to send a callback.

success_criteria: The correct Execute AGV command is accessed in the Aveva AGV command area.
The full AGV ID for the already-completed task is specified.
The AGV task is completed in software after the missed callback condition.
healthy_conditions: The affected AGV is correctly identified as having already completed its task physically.
The full AGV ID is known and entered correctly.
The software reflects completion of the AGV task after command execution.
failure_conditions: The AGVs Aveva API page or Execute AGV command cannot be accessed.
The correct AGV cannot be identified.
The full AGV ID is not known or is uncertain.
The command does not complete the AGV task as expected.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the correct AGV ID is not known.
Escalate if the command does not complete the task as expected.
If restart-related recovery is being considered in the broader incident, preserve the source safety note that the system should be E-stopped before doing a restart.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGVs Aveva API page and listed AGV recovery functions, including Execute AGV command.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: In the event of an RMS crash, Execute AGV command enables users to specify an AGV ID that has completed its task when RMS fails to send a callback.

missing_details: The exact Aveva navigation path is not provided in the source.
The exact button labels and field labels for Execute AGV command are not provided in the source.
The source does not provide confirmation text, response messages, or explicit verification steps after execution.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this procedure.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required for this action.
